origin.name: Rwanda
origin.key_document: Death certificate from local authority (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Rwanda travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Kigali guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Rwanda is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from local authority (English language)
special_rules: Rwanda issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance from the Rwanda National Police is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Rwandan authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Rwandan documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Kigali can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Rwanda to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Rwanda. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Local registration, police clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
